curatedFacts.founded: 1855, named after London, England; known as 'The Forest City'
curatedFacts.landmarks: Covent Garden Market, Banting House, Eldon House, Fanshawe Pioneer Village, Grand Theatre, Museum London
curatedFacts.notable: known as 'The Forest City' — a university city with strong healthcare and education sectors driving innovation and growth
curatedFacts.economy: education (Western University, Fanshawe College), healthcare (London Health Sciences Centre), insurance, digital media, food processing
curatedFacts.entityDesc: London is a city in Southwestern Ontario, Ontario, Canada, with a population of approximately 422,324. Education (western university, fanshawe college), healthcare (london health sciences centre), insurance, digital media, food processing.
curatedFacts.longDesc:
London isn't just Ontario's city — it's a place where ambition meets opportunity. Founded named after London, England; known as 'The Forest City', this city has transformed into a thriving community of over ~420,000 people.

What makes London remarkable for {BUSINESS_TYPE}? Scale and diversity. The city encompasses everything from Victoria Park, Storybook Gardens, Western Fair, vibrant Old East Village, Thames River corridor, London Music Hall of Fame. Each neighborhood has its own character, its own needs, and its own expectations.

The local economy is driven by education (Western University, Fanshawe College), healthcare (London Health Sciences Centre), insurance, digital media, food processing. This economic foundation creates steady demand for services and supports a community that takes pride in maintaining its properties and infrastructure.

Community features include Covent Garden Market, Banting House, Eldon House, Fanshawe Pioneer Village, Grand Theatre, Museum London, which give London its distinctive character.

London benefits from its location in Southwestern Ontario. Whether you need routine {SERVICE} maintenance or a major project, {COMPANY_NAME} understands what London properties need. We've invested the time to learn this community — the local building codes, the weather patterns, the neighbourhood expectations. That's why London residents keep choosing {COMPANY_NAME}.
